metadata.scope: release readiness checklist for internal operations and leadpack delivery
source_documents: D11_测试验收与金标回归清单.md
D2_正式对象契约与字段字典.md
D3_正式规则码总表与判定说明书.md
D6_字段策略字典与客户交付字段规范.md
D7_对象级交付矩阵与外发治理规范.md
D13_公开可查边界能力清单.md

items.text: L0 working master, D1-D14 and 裁决总表 use one vocabulary.
No duplicate formal D13 or duplicate top-level decision source exists.
All formal decision ids referenced in docs exist in 裁决总表.
LeadPack SKU A/B/C/D is treated as commercial packaging, not capability tier A/B/C/D.
schema_catalog, enum_catalog, rule_catalog and gate_policies are present and aligned.
No schema or enum introduces a second state vocabulary or object name.
Governance and release machine assets exist and use the same ids as docs.
Delivery writeback and governance feedback records are required for leadpack delivery.
Stage 9 exception taxonomy catalogs and rules are present (payment/delivery/outcome/governance).

sections.title: 双闸门、边界与外发阻断

items.text: Dual-gate upgrade matrix matches D3 and blocks formal upgrade when any gate is not PASS.
Any D-tier derived object or field is blocked from leadpack delivery, demo and external platform surfaces.
Leadpack/demo release never relies solely on screenshot, OCR text or model summary.
Leadpack delivery requires evidence, review, gate and approval with full audit chain.
Sales consumption gate enforces internal-only visibility when leadpack delivery is blocked.
Release layers (DEV_ALLOWED/INTERNAL_OPERABLE/LEADPACK_DELIVERABLE/EXTERNAL_BLOCKED) are consistent across boundary registry, delivery matrix and release gates.
Legacy release token EXTERNAL_LEADPACK_DELIVERABLE is absent from formal docs/contracts/control export and approval surfaces.
External release remains BLOCKED; internal operable does not equal external software release.
LeadPack deliverable requires approval chain and audit chain before delivery.

sections.title: 高限制字段、触达与合规

items.text: Natural-person high restriction fields require lawful basis, approval chain, frequency policy, opt-out and quiet-hours checks.
contact_target and touch_record do not execute when outreach gate blocks.
No raw high-restriction contact value appears in demo or leadpack delivery surfaces.
Stage 8 compliance decision fields (legal basis/frequency/quiet hours/opt-out/channel policy/reasonable expectation) remain internal/governance only and never enter sales or leadpack surfaces.
Model governance record, model tier and human review requirements are aligned with D14.
Release manifest, rollback plan and governance snapshot are prepared for target environment.
All mandatory regression suites in regression_manifest have passed.
deployment_matrix and runtime_policy_catalog are present and aligned with D12 runtime tiers.
model_catalog, model_usage_policy, prompt_policy_catalog, eval_suite_catalog and output_target_matrix are present.
Model outputs are blocked from formal external delivery objects without review and release gates.

sections.title: 经营主链外发与交付门

items.text: LeadPack quality gate is satisfied before any leadpack export.
Sales usage gate requires quality gate, delivery matrix and approvals to be satisfied.
Outreach compliance gate (lawful basis, quiet hours, approval) is enforced before any outreach execution.
channel_family enum and contact_channel_catalog are aligned with D9 formal values.
contact_legal_basis enum aligns with contact_compliance_matrix and contact_source_policy_catalog.
Compliance matrix decisions (ALLOW_PREVIEW/REVIEW_REQUIRED/BLOCKED) are enforced; restricted channel families force review.

Stage 8 execution policies (priority/cadence/retry/stop/feedback) are present and enforced; contact_target/outreach_plan/touch_record typed fields cover opportunity/source/priority/conflict/attempt/writeback/governed preview metadata before outreach execution.
Negotiation, payment, delivery and audit gates are satisfied; email send does not equal delivery completion.
Result writeback completeness gate is satisfied (delivery_record + outcome + governance feedback).
Stage 6 formally emits project_fact/report_record/legal_action_recommendation/challenger_candidate_profile and H-06 carries the Stage 7 actor/buyer minimum seed fields.
Stage 7 consumes Stage 6 formal inputs and blocks/reviews when H-06 critical payload fields are missing instead of privately补造关键主输入。
Stage 7 offer/saleable outputs remain internal-only minimal closure; they are not treated as fully ready external commercial flow.
Stage 7 formally emits multi_competitor_collection and H-07 carries minimal formal refs (collection id / winning candidate id / winning challenger profile id) for Stage 8 consumption.

Stage 8 formally emits contact_candidate_collection and contact_selection_trace before finalizing contact_target, and wrong_role/invalid_contact or explicit reselection reasons persist reselect_history.

sections.title: 阶段 9 商务交付与回写

items.text: Stage 9 rule codes (ORD/PAY/DEL/OUT/GOV) are present and enforced; payment_proof_state, approval or audit missing must block.
Stage 9 field policy covers payment/delivery/outcome/governance fields and aligns with delivery matrix.
Order/payment/delivery/workbench APIs are internal preview only; external delivery remains blocked.
Outcome/governance writeback, typed writeback_targets and archival/retention requirements are satisfied before closure.

Stage 9 direct objects remain internal governed: draft/preview/internal writeback allowed, typed delivery_form/customer_ack/partial/redelivery state retained, direct leadpack or external delivery blocked.
Stage 9 key fields declare review-required and approval/audit conditions before any ready/received/delivered/closure state is used.
Order/payment/delivery APIs and workbench panels only implement order draft, payment draft, delivery preview, outcome writeback and governance feedback writeback semantics.
Archived/retrieval failure, partial delivery, rejected delivery and payment mismatch still force review or block and never allow closure.
Stage 9 delivery matrix and release gates agree on projection-only customer summaries and keep external software release blocked.
Stage 7/8/9 internal surfaces expose preview/draft-only envelopes that consume formal objects, decision states and traces without recomputing main judgment.
Stage 8/9 internal surfaces keep live_execution_enabled=false and blocked_by_default=true; external release remains blocked.
page_surface_states/review_action_catalog/workbench/button flow/export template remain aligned for internal-only preview and draft-only review flows.
source vendor classification is frozen and consistent across enum_catalog, vendor registry, source usage policy, contact schemas and contact catalogs.
execution vendor classification is frozen in enum/schema and execution policies; real execution remains blocked or approval-gated by policy.
MODEL_PROVIDER and TOOL_PROVIDER remain unbound while provider_type, trace requirements and governance-object承接 are frozen.
Model stage signature matrix exists and keeps final decisions prohibited for stages 3-9.
Source/vendor/provider trace and audit fields are reserved in schema_catalog and policy catalogs, and any missing trace forces review or block.

Stage 8 minimal behavior regressions cover contact priority/conflict, cadence, retry, stop condition, quiet-hours/frequency/approval-gap split, retry exhaustion, and failure/writeback readiness; run-golden directly executes the Stage 8 failure/writeback and H-08 payload goldens.

Stage 9 minimal behavior regressions cover CHARGEBACK_REVIEW, PARTIAL_DELIVERY, DELIVERY_REJECTED, ACK_TIMEOUT plus payment/delivery/outcome/governance taxonomy behavior, and run-golden directly executes the Stage 9 consumer/executor/writeback goldens.
H-08 handoff required payload fields and Stage 8 producer set are machine-checkable and aligned before route-map start, and validate-contracts independently asserts the producer/consumer key set.

Stage 8/9 executor consumption is machine-checkable: traceable decisions exist, Stage 9 one-chain executor covers payment_exception/delivery_exception/outcome/governance policies, and validate-contracts/check-semantic-alignment independently assert policy sequence + additive writeback contract semantics.

Stage 9 typed workflow persists H-08 optional preview/writeback fields when present (plan_status/touch_record_state/feedback_reason/written_back_at_optional/governance_decision_state) while staying INTERNAL_GOVERNED and projection-only.

Formal sink quantitative fields (project/opportunity value, price intermediate fields, confidence score, current action start/deadline) are aligned across D2, schema/schema_catalog, H-06/H-07 optional payload, Stage7 projection trace, Stage8 consumption trace and semantic anti-drift checks.

Stage 8 persisted governed preview/draft-only surfaces remain internal_only + blocked_by_default + live_execution_enabled=false + INTERNAL_GOVERNED after create_touch_record/list replay, and replay governed_context keeps writeback plus human handoff metadata machine-readable.

Stage 8 operator queue persists request_review/approve/deny/hold/return progression with assignment lifecycle, pending actions, last_action/action_history, and repository readback keeps persisted refs/governed metadata precedence without creating a second decision system.
runtime_inventory/runtime_policy/model_release_manifest declare capability_mode priority, action intents and decision matrix for runtime permission resolution.
Stage 8 and Stage 9 risky paths resolve capability permissions before policy execution and emit permission_trace + additive governance payload.
EMERGENCY_OFF and PERMANENTLY_BLOCKED short-circuit Stage 8/9 runtime paths with BLOCK semantics.
REAL_RUN_READY remains subordinate to EXTERNAL_BLOCKED and never replaces release_level semantics.
service hardcode anti-regression is active: stage6-9 services still route through shared runtime and do not regrow private governance loops.
governance assets remain behaviorally consumed in Stage 8/9 runtime, not merely present in catalogs or scripts.
schema/model/api drift anti-regression is active for critical Stage7-9 and shared-runtime dependent objects.
Stage 9 second loop anti-regression is active: no private _run_policy_chain, no direct runtime.executor.execute, and permission/governance/semantic traces remain present.

unused catalog / partial consumption guard is active: critical runtime catalogs are explicitly classified as FULL_RUNTIME / INTENTIONAL_PARTIAL / UNDECLARED_PARTIAL / UNUSED, and only declared INTENTIONAL_PARTIAL with a reason may pass.

governance asset runtime consumption closure is active: Stage 1/2 authoritative baseline plus Stage 8/9 permission_trace, governance_trace and Stage 9 writeback contracts prove runtime consumption rather than catalog-only presence.
service hardcode / resolver bypass / second loop guard is active: stage7-9 services do not instantiate private runtime chains or bypass the unified spine.
schema/catalog/runtime-validator/model drift guard is active for Stage 1/2 baseline objects, critical Stage7-9 objects, and shared-runtime dependent enums.
H-01/H-06/H-07/H-08 producer/consumer drift guard is active: producer set, required payload projection, criticalObjects and optional runtime consumer access must match real runtime behavior.

unified runtime spine guard is active: Stage 7 keeps handoff -> policy executor -> semantic order, and Stage 8/9 risky paths keep permission resolver -> policy executor -> governance guard -> semantic validator order with behavior-level permission/policy/governance/semantic traces present.

control/current_task.yaml carries a machine-readable task_packet with objective/non_goals/changed paths/allowed paths/forbidden paths/required scripts/stop conditions/definition of done/deliverables/change_class/review fields.
Declared change_class is not lower than the review_gate_matrix classification derived from touched asset domains.
MANDATORY_HUMAN_REVIEW batches declare human_review_required=true and include all required owner reviews/signoff metadata.
STOP_AND_ESCALATE domains fail readiness/release checks immediately and cannot continue as direct or draft batches.
automation stop conditions explicitly link missing task packet, missing review, wrong change class and stop/escalate domains to release failure.

Machine-readable external unlock prerequisite matrix exists and covers external software release, leadpack external delivery, stage8/stage9 live execution, provider externalization and external export surface domains.

Each future unlock domain declares evidence_pack_required, required_test_suites, required_release_checks, required_owner_signoffs, required_control_assets, required_runtime_modes, required_shadow_or_canary_state and unlock_denial_conditions.
Release/control/model manifests reference the prerequisite matrix and keep external release blocked until a separate future unlock decision batch passes all prerequisites.
Future external unlock decision assets are separate from prerequisite assets; decision-layer assets are MANDATORY_HUMAN_REVIEW while activation/go-live assets remain STOP_AND_ESCALATE.
Leadpack external delivery remains approval-and-audit gated; prerequisite completion does not convert direct Stage8/9 objects into default external deliverables.
External export surfaces remain projection-only; direct object export and restricted-field leakage remain denied by default.
Stage 8 live execution remains blocked by default and may only be discussed after shadow/canary, rollback and kill-switch prerequisites are machine-readable and satisfied.
Stage 8 future unlock still requires high-restriction contact release, runtime guardrails and replayable audit evidence.
Stage 9 live payment execution remains blocked by default and requires shadow/canary, rollback, emergency off and explicit owner signoff prerequisites.
Stage 9 live payment unlock is denied when payer/amount/refund/audit controls are not replayable or reversible.
Stage 9 live delivery execution remains blocked by default and requires shadow/canary, rollback, emergency off and explicit owner signoff prerequisites.
Stage 9 live delivery unlock is denied when archive/retrieval/acknowledgement/exception writeback controls are not replayable or reversible.
Model provider externalized usage remains buildable-but-off and requires external shadow/boundary eval suites plus model governance signoff.
Model outputs remain blocked from client/external surfaces when output_target_matrix still blocks the requested targets, regardless of runtime capability mode.
Tool provider externalized usage remains SHADOW_ONLY and never default-open; direct external mutation paths are denied until a later explicit decision batch.
Source/vendor externalized usage remains denied for personal, private or unauthorized channels even when future unlock prerequisites are otherwise discussed.
Coverage or public boundary state alone never authorizes future unlock; prerequisite evidence pack and owner signoff remain mandatory.
Future unlock prerequisite completion does not itself unlock external release; a later separate unlock decision batch is still required.
Future unlock decision matrix exists and classifies every prerequisite domain into FUTURE_UNLOCK_CANDIDATE / DENY_BY_DEFAULT_CONTINUE / LONG_TERM_BLOCKED_OR_NEVER_DEFAULT_OPEN.
R6 decision sequence order is machine-readable, keeps leadpack/source-vendor/projection paths ahead of live execution/payment, and does not treat any candidate as implementation-approved.
release/control/model manifests reference the R6 decision matrix/state and keep external software release plus Stage 8/9 live execution blocked after the decision batch.
review_gate_matrix distinguishes decision-layer assets (MANDATORY_HUMAN_REVIEW) from unlock activation/go-live assets (STOP_AND_ESCALATE).
Domains marked DENY_BY_DEFAULT_CONTINUE or LONG_TERM_BLOCKED_OR_NEVER_DEFAULT_OPEN must fail any attempt to claim unlock implementation readiness.
Stage 7/8/9 formal objects have repository boundaries that persist formal payloads, ids, refs, decision states and trace/audit metadata without recomputing main judgment.
Stage 7/8/9 preview surfaces can read repository-backed internal state after writeback/draft creation, instead of requiring transient StageBundle-only inputs.

Stage 8/9 writeback fields (written_back_at_optional, writeback_targets, exception-specific additive targets, governed metadata, decision states) survive persistence and remain internal-only / governed-only.

Repository/storage code does not become a second decision system: saleability, governance decision, capability mode and payment/delivery outcome remain produced upstream by formal runtime/state flows.

Machine-readable leadpack_external_delivery candidate matrix exists and declares candidate_status/candidate_scope/projection_only/direct_object_export_allowed/required approvals/audit refs/boundary checks/masking rules/denial conditions/why-not-live/why-not-now/future activation prerequisites.
Candidate projection boundary explicitly distinguishes allowed projection, masked projection, summary-only and forbidden for Stage 7/8/9 objects, and keeps direct Stage 8/9 object export blocked.

Leadpack candidate preview/export simulation surfaces are internal-only, candidate-only, repository-backed, require review, keep external_delivery_enabled=false and never trigger real external delivery.
API/UI catalogs expose candidate preview, governed review request, export simulation, hold and denial actions, and permission matrix keeps all candidate resources internal-only.

Historical candidate-gap assets keep READY_FOR_POST-R6_CANDIDATE_GAP_BATCH as the decision-time token only; it must not remain the current repo readiness or imply activation-prep/unlock implementation.
Leadpack candidate gap keeps capability/governance/semantic/review-gate guardrails active and does not create a second decision system.

Stage 1-2 authoritative baseline is machine-readable and aligned across docs/contracts/handoff/schemas/services: source family/platform/carrier baseline is no longer sample-only, route baseline covers major route types, H-01 keeps source/route/carrier authority, and version/clock/deadline baseline is persisted on Stage 2 objects.
sale_gate_status uses the canonical OPEN/REVIEW/HOLD/BLOCK vocabulary across L0, enums, runtime and tests; ALLOW is retired.
shared runtime validator enforces schema type/shape/additionalProperties for Stage 1-5 critical objects instead of relying only on late-stage strict profiles.

writeback impact executor is explicitly marked INTERNAL_V0_ACTIVE in docs/contracts/control/testing; writeback_target_contracts define target family/mutation/persistence semantics, typed writeback plus internal-only additive target mutation projection exist, and direct target persistence or any live execution remain blocked.
repo_status/current_task/milestone/status_board/opening_decision_page/release_manifest/model_release_manifest all carry the same canonical readiness token READY_FOR_POST-REPAIR_MAINLINE_SELECTION.
candidate-gap/strategic-branch/closure-review/mainline-selection are expressed as layered state dimensions; none of the first three may remain the current repo readiness token.

Stage 7/8/9 surfaces must distinguish transient_preview_context from persisted_operational_context, and only persisted loops may expose work_item, assignment lifecycle, pending actions, last action, and action history.

Stage 7/8/9 operator action routes only update repository-backed work item and action history; they must not re-judge saleability, governance, capability mode, taxonomy, or release gate conclusions, and must consume review_action_catalog/button_flow_catalog semantics.
Stage 8/9 writeback draft progression, review, denial, hold, return, and completion states are persisted and replayable through internal-only operator loops.
Repository-backed stage7/stage8/stage9 work item queues use durable local persistence, survive default-session reopen, and remain free of any second decision system.

Operator queue assignment must expose formal assignment lifecycle states (unassigned/assigned/in_review/returned/completed/denied) and a work-item key that does not collapse concurrent stage items by project alone.

Operator runtime must return structured WORKITEM-404-NOT_FOUND, ACTION-409-NOT_PENDING, ACTION-409-AUDIT_REQUIRED, and ACTION-409-APPROVAL_REQUIRED envelopes instead of ValueError, synthetic audit backfill, or synthetic approval backfill.
Internal product operationalization keeps external software release and Stage 8/9 live execution blocked-by-default with no activation-prep or external unlock side effects.

Machine-readable leadpack activation prep evidence pack exists and declares evidence_pack_status, required_evidence_items, evidence_item_sources, evidence_item_freshness, replay/approval/audit/boundary requirements, denial conditions and remaining blockers.
Leadpack activation prep runbook formalizes deny, hold, return for revision, rollback/cancel prep, stale or missing evidence, missing approval or audit, and boundary violation paths.
Export simulation replay is attached as a formal activation-prep artifact with replay status, boundary verdicts, masking/projection checks, missing approval or audit refs and trace bundle references.
Leadpack activation prep signoff packet exists, lists all mandatory owner roles, allows draft packet only, and explicitly states draft signoff is not activation-ready.

Candidate-specific review gate and transition matrix distinguish candidate gap, activation prep in progress, activation prep ready for review, held and denied, without claiming activation-ready or external-ready.

Leadpack activation prep APIs and UI surfaces remain internal-only, candidate-only and projection-only; review requests must fail formally when prep evidence, replay, signoff packet or boundary checks are incomplete.

Leadpack activation design / implementation prep matrix exists and keeps scope design-prep only, with actual activation, external delivery, external software release, live execution and direct object export disabled.

Owner signoff execution states are actual state-backed, distinguish declared/requested/approved/denied/held/expired/withdrawn, require all mandatory owners, and map missing or denied owners to HOLD or NO-GO.
Approval chain, review gate, audit refs and owner signoff prerequisites are machine-readable separate sources; review gates must not appear as approval-chain items.
Activation prep, GO/NO-GO/HOLD decision, activation design prep and implementation decision states are formally separated without changing canonical repo readiness.
Activation-specific cancel, rollback and emergency-off design controls exist with trigger conditions, owner authority and negative states.

Implementation-prep readiness gate requires release checks, regression suites, actual owner signoff states, approval chains, review gates and audit refs; missing approval/audit/review/signoff sources must HOLD before any implementation decision.

Implementation decision readiness packet is machine-readable and review-only, summarizes owner signoff, approval chain, review gate, audit refs and merged HOLD sources, and explicitly keeps implementation_decision_ready=false while blockers remain.

Formal sink quantitative fields exist in D2, concrete schemas, schema_catalog, H-06/H-07 optional payload fields, semantic alignment checks, and runtime projection/consumption traces without changing release/capability/vendor/provider semantics.

sections.title: Blueprint Registry / FF-17 Regression Release Hardening

items.text: current_task.task_packet.source_blueprint_batch_id is accepted only when the value is registered in control/task_packet_library.yaml#blueprint_registry; B0-B10 remain legacy historical-compatible entries, FF-01~FF-18 and POST-FF-* registered entries are valid, and unregistered values such as UNKNOWN-BATCH are release-blocking.

FF-17-S1 complex negative runtime goldens (INVALID_CONTACT, ORG_ROUTED H-08 payload, PARTIAL_PAYMENT, PAYER_MISMATCH) are treated as release-blocking checks through existing runtime_check_id-backed run-golden coverage.

Stage 7/8/9 internal surfaces and operator flows remain internal_only + draft-only/preview-only + blocked_by_default=true + live_execution_enabled=false; operator progression or persisted replay does not imply live execution or external delivery.

Stage 9 typed workflow/writeback layering remains formal and governed: outcome base is authoritative, governance/payment/delivery targets stay additive, projected-only/advisory-only targets remain separated, and persisted writeback metadata stays internal-governed only.

contracts/release/runtime_policy_catalog.json remains the sole canonical source for capability mode, priority order, protected short-circuit and EXTERNAL_BLOCKED redline; runtime_inventory, model_release_manifest and release_manifest remain projection/mirror surfaces only.

future unlock / candidate / design-prep / implementation-decision assets do not redefine current repo readiness or external release; post-FF control modernization does not auto-activate the next packet and may proceed only after all required scripts pass.
check-release.ps1 must include doctor.ps1 as the umbrella preflight, so runtime/tooling/repo-root prerequisites are checked before the release entrypoint can pass.

check-release.ps1 must include check-handoff-dependencies.ps1 in the same umbrella flow, so handoff dependency order, root assets and required files remain release-blocking under the total entrypoint.
